name: Maple Cloud Cake
recipeInstructions:
For the cake:
1. Boil the maple syrup for exactly 2 minutes.
2. Beat the egg whites until stiff and set aside.
3. Beat the egg yolks and vanilla until light, then gradually mix in the boiled syrup.
4. Gently fold the egg yolk mixture into the egg whites.
5. Sift the remaining dry ingredients together three times and fold them into the egg mixture.
6. Pour the batter into an ungreased 9 or 10-inch tube pan.
7. Bake at 325 degrees F for 50 minutes, or until done. When the cake is cooked, invert the pan on a cake rack and let cool for 1 hour.
For the icing:
1. Boil the maple syrup until it forms a firm ball when tested in ice water (328 degrees F on a candy thermometer).
2. Beat the egg whites until stiff.
3. Slowly pour the hot syrup onto the beaten egg whites, beating constantly with an electric beater.
4. If using a hand rotary beater, pour in a little syrup, beat, pour more, and continue beating until all syrup is used. Continue beating until the frosting stands in peaks or cools.
5. Spread on top and sides of the cake.
